title: OAuth, Secrets, Identity, and Least Privilege · Spiral Review
summary: Secure a remote MCP server serving user-specific data. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an OAuth and credential-handling design after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Decide when authentication and user authorization are required by data and action scope.
Implement protected resource metadata and OAuth-aligned authorization for remote HTTP.
Validate token audience, issuer, scopes, expiry, and resource binding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

stimulus.title: Least Privilege Tools case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: agent ext authorization. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Read-only tool names guarantee read-only implementation.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map scopes and approvals to narrow tools and user intent. Artifact under review: an OAuth and credential-handling design.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Least Privilege Tools, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Least Privilege Tools applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Map scopes and approvals to narrow tools and user intent.
